title: Double Double Boy In Trouble

jokes.text: Stupid shopping list, turning food into work.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about mundane task
jokes.punchline: Reframes shopping as an unreasonable burden
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer lazy attitude, economical phrasing

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm doin' something!
jokes.setup: Child demanding attention in public
jokes.punchline: The vague, meaningless nature of what he's announcing he's doing
jokes.explanation: Standard bratty kid behavior - not particularly clever but character-appropriate

jokes.text: You better settle down, boy, or I'll drag you into the blind spot of that security camera and beat you with one of those big thick women's magazines with all the ads in it!
jokes.setup: Parent threatening misbehaving child
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific threat involving security camera blind spots and magazine selection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mild threat to weirdly specific criminal planning. The magazine detail is perfect Homer logic.

jokes.text: They say the last one is lucky! Unlike all the others.
jokes.setup: Typical lottery ticket sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts his own sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure - builds expectation then immediately deflates it. Great economy of words.

jokes.text: Ponder this, Homer. This ticket would have been yours if your kid hadn't been screwin' around.
jokes.setup: Lenny wins lottery with ticket Homer almost bought
jokes.punchline: Twists knife by pointing out Homer's bad luck
jokes.explanation: Solid situational irony, creates cringe for Homer

jokes.text: You just cost me $200,000! - I thought it was 50. - I was gonna bet it on the dogs!
jokes.setup: Homer angry about lost lottery money
jokes.punchline: Reveals he would have immediately gambled it away
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - defeats his own argument by revealing he'd waste the money anyway

jokes.text: All the groceries seem to have been balled up in anger.
jokes.setup: Homer's rage at losing lottery money
jokes.punchline: Marge's clinical observation of destroyed groceries
jokes.explanation: Marge's understated delivery contrasts with the chaos, 'balled up in anger' is a perfect phrase

jokes.text: That kid has become a Dennis-level menace!
jokes.setup: Homer describing Bart's troublemaking
jokes.punchline: References Dennis the Menace comic strip
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pop culture reference, works for character but not very original

jokes.text: Lenny, Lenny, he's our guy! Got the ticket Homer didn't buy!
jokes.setup: Bar patrons celebrating Lenny's win
jokes.punchline: Cheerleader-style chant rubbing salt in Homer's wound
jokes.explanation: Good rhythm and rhyme, adds to Homer's misery

jokes.text: I don't want your pity booze. I'll pay for it myself. / Nope, Lucky Lenny's buying all the drinks tonight.
jokes.setup: Homer refusing Lenny's generosity
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercut by Moe's refusal to let him pay
jokes.explanation: Homer can't even maintain his dignity - bar won't let him pay

jokes.text: Don't waste your shot, Bart. You've got just enough cat pee to soak two dorks.
jokes.setup: Nelson giving tactical advice
jokes.punchline: Reveals Bart is using cat urine as ammunition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - treating disgusting prank like military strategy

jokes.text: Uh... it was water this morning.
jokes.setup: Adult asking if Bart sprayed water
jokes.punchline: Implies the water has become something else by now
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - implies gross transformation without stating it. Perfect Bart logic.

jokes.text: Ha ha! You feel self-conscious.
jokes.setup: Marge feeling bad about backup dress
jokes.punchline: Bart gleefully pointing out her insecurity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Bart being cruel with childlike directness

jokes.text: Well, Marge, you did have that one sip of alcohol while you were pregnant.
jokes.setup: Marge wondering why Bart misbehaves
jokes.punchline: Homer blames microscopic amount of prenatal drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - absurd victim blaming over trivial incident

jokes.text: They're not boogers! They're clumps of rubber cement. That I stored in my nose.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about Bart flicking boogers
jokes.punchline: Bart's 'correction' makes it even more disgusting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant - attempts to defend himself but makes it worse. The pause before 'That I stored in my nose' is perfect.

jokes.text: Stop it, both of you! Don't make me drive into that tree. You know I will!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to control fighting kids
jokes.punchline: Threatens vehicular suicide as discipline
jokes.explanation: Incredible escalation from normal parental threat to something completely unhinged. 'You know I will!' sells it perfectly.

jokes.text: In the beginning, there was darkness, so God created man. But man was alone. God created the animals and birds. But man was still lonely. So God said, 'Let there be friends!'
jokes.setup: Lenny giving speech at his party
jokes.punchline: Parodies Biblical creation story to celebrate friendship
jokes.explanation: Creative concept, good structure building to friendship theme

jokes.text: These are going right on my mom's grave.
jokes.setup: Carl taking home centerpiece flowers
jokes.punchline: Reveals morbid destination for party decorations
jokes.explanation: Dark turn delivered matter-of-factly, good economy

jokes.text: These innocent robots couldn't have done this on their own. Someone would've had to turn them all on at once, and then imbue them with some kind of malevolent sentience.
jokes.setup: Wiggum investigating robot mayhem
jokes.punchline: Overcomplicated explanation for simple prank
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum - overthinking simple situation with pseudo-scientific language

jokes.text: Well, whoever did this must be long gone by now, Chief. I don't know how we'll ever catch him. / Kill! Kill! Kill! / Hold it right there, Bart!
jokes.setup: Lou saying culprit is long gone
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately identified by murderous robot voice
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - incompetent police work immediately undermined by evidence

jokes.text: Bart make Bot bad!
jokes.setup: Robot being questioned about crime
jokes.punchline: Speaks in broken English like caveman
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - robot witness speaks primitively

jokes.text: What? You can't take my Balderdash!
jokes.setup: Homer taking away board games as punishment
jokes.punchline: Bart's horror at losing word game
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - troublemaker cares about intellectual game

jokes.text: I don't know why I do the things I do. No one understands me. I wish I had a different family. / I feel exactly the same way.
jokes.setup: Bart having existential crisis about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Rich kid echoing same sentiment despite different circumstances
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode premise - both kids unhappy despite different situations

jokes.text: Terrific? Incredible? Happy as a clown? / Oh, yeah, I'm livin' the dream.
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting how rich people feel
jokes.punchline: Simon's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Standard rich-kid-isn't-happy trope, competent delivery

jokes.text: Get out here, boy! I want to punish you before I get drunk and merciful! / Deal.
jokes.setup: Homer calling for Bart to be punished
jokes.punchline: Reveals his punishment window is limited by drinking schedule
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - admits alcohol makes him lenient. Perfect character integration and timing.

jokes.text: There you are, Master Simon! / Thank you, uh, Lipizzaner.
jokes.setup: Butler greeting who he thinks is Simon
jokes.punchline: Bart calls him by horse breed name instead of Chester
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart confused by wealth, mixing up horse and human names

jokes.text: McMansion. McMansion. McMansion. McDonald's? McDreamy. McSteamy. McMansion. Fleetwood Mac, and Macauley Culkin.
jokes.setup: Driver giving directions using 'Mc' prefix
jokes.punchline: Expands to include unrelated 'Mac/Mc' references
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay concept but feels random, not well integrated

jokes.text: A candy corn volcano! A ceiling from Saddam's palace! Ooh, and a racecar bed. That really races!
jokes.setup: Bart discovering luxurious bedroom
jokes.punchline: Mix of ridiculous wealth indicators, ending with functional race car bed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of wealth absurdity, Saddam reference dates it

jokes.text: I'm the real deal. Every day I stand here the family donates a million dollars to Notre Dame. Did you know the words 'Notre Dame' are French, but the team is the Fighting Irish? That's the kind of thing I think about in here.
jokes.setup: Bart thinks he's looking at poster
jokes.punchline: Real Joe Montana trapped, obsessing over linguistic inconsistencies
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd concept - celebrity held captive, forced into trivial observations

jokes.text: 'Blocked Number'? That service is $3.65 a month.
jokes.setup: Milhouse seeing caller ID from rich kid
jokes.punchline: Focuses on cost of phone service instead of mystery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - missing the point, focused on mundane details

jokes.text: I made it with Cheetos, just like you like it. If the sauce is too thick, I can add more root beer.
jokes.setup: Marge serving dinner to 'Simon'
jokes.punchline: Recipe combining junk food in bizarre way
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination presented as normal cooking, good Marge character work

jokes.text: Keep your meal in your mouth, you semiliterate spew-monkey.
jokes.setup: Homer spitting food while talking
jokes.punchline: Overly sophisticated insult from 'Bart'
jokes.explanation: 'Spew-monkey' is brilliantly crude yet sophisticated. Shows character switch perfectly.

jokes.text: Keep your meal in your mouth, you semiliterate spew-monkey.
jokes.setup: Homer spitting food while talking
jokes.punchline: Pretentious insult combining sophisticated and crude language
jokes.explanation: Great insult construction - mixing high and low vocabulary perfectly

jokes.text: If that boy thinks I'm paying to put him through four years of puberty, forget it. I'm made of hormones.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Bart's strange behavior
jokes.punchline: Treats puberty like college, claims to be literally made of hormones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - misunderstands puberty as financial burden, bizarre self-description

jokes.text: So I said, 'Dad, I wanted a Range Rover, not a Land Rover. This is the worst Martin Luther King Day ever.'
jokes.setup: Rich kid complaining about gift
jokes.punchline: Trivial luxury problem connected to civil rights holiday
jokes.explanation: Sharp contrast between privilege and historical significance, good character work

jokes.text: The old hot spoon. But why? / Because you're our half-brother, you're blocking our full inheritance and we hate you.
jokes.setup: Siblings torturing Bart with hot spoon
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of inheritance-motivated cruelty
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical - establishes stakes and motivations clearly

jokes.text: For your information, The Daily Growl is the only newspaper that's not afraid to say how great this country is.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his newspaper choice
jokes.punchline: Admits preference for purely positive propaganda
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character work - prefers comforting lies to reality

jokes.text: For your information, The Daily Growl is the only newspaper that's not afraid to say how great this country is.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his newspaper choice
jokes.punchline: Admitting he only reads positive propaganda
jokes.explanation: Good satire of confirmation bias and propaganda consumption

jokes.text: How's the war going? We're winning? That's great. And our standing abroad? Fantastic.
jokes.setup: Homer reading his propaganda newspaper
jokes.punchline: Unrealistically positive news reports
jokes.explanation: Straightforward media bias joke - competent but not inspired

jokes.text: This is like a rap video before the rappers show up.
jokes.setup: Homer at fancy party
jokes.punchline: Compares to rap video without the rappers
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about luxury settings, good economy

jokes.text: Fancy party. This is like a rap video before the rappers show up.
jokes.setup: Bart observing wealthy party atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Comparing to rap video aesthetic without the performers
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural observation but feels like easy pop culture reference

jokes.text: Do you know that after a hundred years dead bodies turn to red licorice? / Whoa-ho, let me at it.
jokes.setup: Devon trying to gross out Bart with fake fact
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic response to eating corpse candy
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Bart's appetite defeats attempt to disgust him

jokes.text: All they had was Sunnytime Pink. / Premix? / I beg your forgiveness, lad. Smithers, pour it down your pants.
jokes.setup: Burns asking for lemonade
jokes.punchline: Extreme punishment for serving premix instead of fresh
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - absurdly excessive punishment for minor beverage infraction

jokes.text: My older brother was trampled by a horse. My sister died of a poisoned potato. My twin was shot. That girl was stabbed. He ate another poisoned potato. Spontaneous combustion. Fell down a well. Potato. Potato, and impaled on the Chrysler Building.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining how his siblings died
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd deaths, many involving poisoned potatoes
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation. The repeated 'potato' becomes increasingly funny, perfect Burns darkness.

jokes.text: Why won't you come down? / I told you I only weigh as much as my clothes and keys.
jokes.setup: Simon trying to get Burns off teeter-totter
jokes.punchline: Burns claims to be essentially weightless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - so old and frail he's basically weightless. Great visual comedy.

jokes.text: You were listening? That means I've got to come up with an ending for this nonsense.
jokes.setup: Grampa telling rambling story
jokes.punchline: Admits he was making it up and now needs conclusion
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy about storytelling itself. Perfect Grampa - rambling without purpose.

jokes.text: It was the war to end all wars, but Pepsi would not give up. They continued to challenge Coke.
jokes.setup: Grampa forced to conclude his war story
jokes.punchline: Turns historical phrase into cola wars narrative
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference mixed with commercial rivalry

jokes.text: Oh. The real Bart would have thrown me out the window. / He would have defenestrated you? / You're just digging yourself even deeper.
jokes.setup: Lisa testing if this is really Bart
jokes.punchline: Simon uses sophisticated vocabulary, further proving he's not Bart
jokes.explanation: 'Defenestrated' is perfect vocabulary test. Lisa's follow-up shows his mistake.

jokes.text: You know, I have never packed before.
jokes.setup: Milhouse telling Bart to pack
jokes.punchline: Bart admits he's never done basic life skill
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Bart's sheltered life despite being troublemaker

jokes.text: You know, I have never packed before.
jokes.setup: Being told to pack quickly for escape
jokes.punchline: Simon's admission of complete inexperience with basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Good wealth privilege joke. Simon has never needed to do anything for himself.

jokes.text: Scrubbing you is easy 'cause you've been pre-rinsed. And when you're covered with goo, that's when I'm scrubbing you.
jokes.setup: Marge singing while bathing Simon
jokes.punchline: Turns mundane bathing into upbeat jingle
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge - making mundane tasks into cheerful songs

jokes.text: So now Bart is on his way to Aspen, where they're going to try to kill him. / Thank you, future Bart, for traveling back through time to warn us.
jokes.setup: Simon explaining the danger Bart is in
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets as time travel scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - can't understand simple explanation, invents sci-fi alternative

jokes.text: Uh, we're champagnos. Who wants a mimosa?
jokes.setup: Homer commenting that even winos look better in Aspen
jokes.punchline: Wino corrects him with upscale terminology and offers brunch cocktail
jokes.explanation: Great class-conscious wordplay. 'Champagnos' is brilliant portmanteau.

jokes.text: Potato?
jokes.setup: After discussing inheritance and murder plans
jokes.punchline: Casual offer of the established murder weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Burns' sibling death list. Single word packs huge comedic punch.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, gravity. You used to be cool.
jokes.setup: Homer falling during rescue attempt
jokes.punchline: Addresses gravity as if it were a friend who betrayed him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anthropomorphization of physics. Perfect Homer - personalizes everything.

jokes.text: Take me with you. I'll be anything and everything you want. Don't leave me here with them!
jokes.setup: Simon realizing wealth isn't worth the family danger
jokes.punchline: Desperate pleading to return to normal family
jokes.explanation: Completes character arc but fairly standard 'grass isn't greener' resolution

tmdb_overview: An irreverent take on the Prince and the Pauper. Bart enthusiastically trades lives with a wealthy lookalike after landing himself in trouble for triggering a rampage of vacuum cleaners, but finds the lavish lifestyle too much when he becomes the target of all his new friends' pranks.
